visa_types.name: A/2 Student Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Required documents:
- Application for a student visa, completed and signed.
- Recent passport photo (against a white background, with full front view of face, measuring 5x5 cm).
- Foreign passport, valid for the period of the visa.
- Confirmation of acceptance to the educational institution.
- Confirmation of payment of tuition.
- Proof of means of subsistence (printout of bank statement for at least three months).
- Birth certificate (no need for an apostille stamp or chain of signatures).
- Medical insurance.
- Proof of residence in Israel.
- Photographs of parents' passports
Declaration Form (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)
Declaration Form (English)
Declaration Form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/israel/moldova/english/pdf/Declaration-template-%D0%A0%D0%A3.pdf) (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)
visa_types.visa_fees:
Recipients of this visa are not permitted to work in Israel.

[A/2 Student Visa > visa fees]
Visa fees payments can only be made in local currency (MDL) by credit card at the VFS Israel Visa Application Centre.
- VFS Service Charges are inclusive of taxes and are non-refundable.
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Consular services
visa_types.processing_time: - Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.
visa_types.forms:
Applicants, who wish to obtain an Israel visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout.
Download Visa Applications Forms 

Please Note:
- Number of pages: 3
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.
Download Instructions:
The forms are in PDF format.
You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms. Please click here to download Adobe Acrobat Reader.
To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is granted to those who want to study in Israel in elementary and high schools, academic institutions, yeshivot and youth institutions of the Jewish Agency. The visa is valid for up to one year and for multiple entrances and exits.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is granted to those who want to study in Israel in elementary and high schools, academic institutions, yeshivot and youth institutions of the Jewish Agency. The visa is valid for up to one year and for multiple entrances and exits.

## Visa Fees
Recipients of this visa are not permitted to work in Israel.

[A/2 Student Visa > visa fees]
Visa fees payments can only be made in local currency (MDL) by credit card at the VFS Israel Visa Application Centre.
- VFS Service Charges are inclusive of taxes and are non-refundable.
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Consular services

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- Application for a student visa, completed and signed.
- Recent passport photo (against a white background, with full front view of face, measuring 5x5 cm).
- Foreign passport, valid for the period of the visa.
- Confirmation of acceptance to the educational institution.
- Confirmation of payment of tuition.
- Proof of means of subsistence (printout of bank statement for at least three months).
- Birth certificate (no need for an apostille stamp or chain of signatures).
- Medical insurance.
- Proof of residence in Israel.
- Photographs of parents' passports
Declaration Form (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)
Declaration Form (English)
Declaration Form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/israel/moldova/english/pdf/Declaration-template-%D0%A0%D0%A3.pdf) (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)

## Photo Specifications
- Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.

## Processing Time
- Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

## Download Forms
Applicants, who wish to obtain an Israel visa application form, can download the requisite form here.
Please note the instructions mentioned below while taking the printout.
Download Visa Applications Forms 

Please Note:
- Number of pages: 3
- You can also obtain this form from the visa application centre.
Download Instructions:
The forms are in PDF format.
You will need an Adobe Acrobat Reader to view and download these forms. Please click here to download Adobe Acrobat Reader.
To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: B1 Employment Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Required documents:
Industry – Construction:
- Visa application form completed and signed.
- Applicant’s passport required validity: two years.
- Original criminal record certificate, apostilled, valid for six months.
- Original birth certificate apostilled.
- Medical certificate for infectious diseases (including HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis A, B, and C). Tests must be conducted within three months prior to submitting the application, with interpretation by a physician from an authorized institution in the country. A signed letter from the doctor explicitly stating that, based on the test results, the worker is fit for work and does not carry or suffer from any infectious disease.
- Declaration of consent to fingerprinting, filled and signed.
- Affidavit signed by the employee indicating that the employee understands the nature of the role he/she will be required to perform.
Nursing / Caregivers:
- Visa application form completed and signed.
- Applicant’s passport required validity: three years.
- Original criminal record certificate apostilled and translated by notary, valid for six months.
- Original birth certificate apostilled and translated by notary.
- Medical certificate for infectious diseases (including HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis A, B, and C, gonorrhea). Tests must be conducted within three months prior to submitting the application, with interpretation by a doctor from an authorized institution in the country. A signed letter from the doctor explicitly stating that, based on the test results, the worker is fit for work and does not carry or suffer from any infectious disease.
- Declaration of consent to fingerprinting, filled and signed.
- Sign an affidavit stating that the worker understands the nature of the position to be performed.
- Lungs x-ray results, to check out tuberculosis.
For the consular interview, the visa applicant must bring:
- Employment contract translated into a language understood by the foreign worker (the applicant), including a translator’s certification and a copy in English or Hebrew, signed by the Israeli employer and the employee, and certified by an Israeli lawyer that the contract complies with the laws of the State of Israel.
- Official certificate from the relevant government ministry in the country of origin confirming the worker has completed nursing training.
- Knowledge of English at conversational level, or the employer’s language in special cases, with proof from a certified test.
For Foreign Expert:
- Completed and signed visa application form.
- Criminal record certificate issued within the last year.
- Medical certificate for infectious diseases as above.
- Passport valid for one year.
Declaration Form (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)
Declaration Form (English)
Declaration Form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/israel/moldova/english/pdf/Declaration-template-%D0%A0%D0%A3.pdf) (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)

Notice on restrictions for foreign caregivers in Israel changing employers.
visa_types.processing_time: - Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A B1 Business Employment Visa is granted to someone who wishes to seek Employment in Israel.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A B1 Business Employment Visa is granted to someone who wishes to seek Employment in Israel.

## Visa Fees And Service Charge

[B1 Employment Visa > visa fees]
Visa fees payments can only be made in local currency (MDL) by credit card at the VFS Israel Visa Application Centre.
- VFS Service Charges are inclusive of taxes and are non-refundable.
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Consular services

## Documents Required
Required documents:
Industry – Construction:
- Visa application form completed and signed.
- Applicant’s passport required validity: two years.
- Original criminal record certificate, apostilled, valid for six months.
- Original birth certificate apostilled.
- Medical certificate for infectious diseases (including HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis A, B, and C). Tests must be conducted within three months prior to submitting the application, with interpretation by a physician from an authorized institution in the country. A signed letter from the doctor explicitly stating that, based on the test results, the worker is fit for work and does not carry or suffer from any infectious disease.
- Declaration of consent to fingerprinting, filled and signed.
- Affidavit signed by the employee indicating that the employee understands the nature of the role he/she will be required to perform.
Nursing / Caregivers:
- Visa application form completed and signed.
- Applicant’s passport required validity: three years.
- Original criminal record certificate apostilled and translated by notary, valid for six months.
- Original birth certificate apostilled and translated by notary.
- Medical certificate for infectious diseases (including HIV, syphilis, and hepatitis A, B, and C, gonorrhea). Tests must be conducted within three months prior to submitting the application, with interpretation by a doctor from an authorized institution in the country. A signed letter from the doctor explicitly stating that, based on the test results, the worker is fit for work and does not carry or suffer from any infectious disease.
- Declaration of consent to fingerprinting, filled and signed.
- Sign an affidavit stating that the worker understands the nature of the position to be performed.
- Lungs x-ray results, to check out tuberculosis.
For the consular interview, the visa applicant must bring:
- Employment contract translated into a language understood by the foreign worker (the applicant), including a translator’s certification and a copy in English or Hebrew, signed by the Israeli employer and the employee, and certified by an Israeli lawyer that the contract complies with the laws of the State of Israel.
- Official certificate from the relevant government ministry in the country of origin confirming the worker has completed nursing training.
- Knowledge of English at conversational level, or the employer’s language in special cases, with proof from a certified test.
For Foreign Expert:
- Completed and signed visa application form.
- Criminal record certificate issued within the last year.
- Medical certificate for infectious diseases as above.
- Passport valid for one year.

visa_types.name: B2 Tourist Visit
visa_types.documents_required:
Required documents:
- A completed and signed visa application form (Form K/1).
- Two recent color photographs, size 5×5 cm, on a white background.
- Original passport of the applicant + a copy of the passport.
- Medical insurance issued in the applicant’s name, covering the entire period of stay in Israel + payment receipt.
- Round-trip flight tickets issued in the applicant’s name.
- Employment certificate for the last months / letter from the employer.
- Bank statement showing account transactions for the last three months.
- Proof of accommodation in Israel:
- 
– hotel reservation, or
- – confirmation of accommodation from friends/relatives (indicating the address in Israel and supporting documents / invitation letter with the address).
- Contact details of relatives or friends in Israel (if applicable).
Consular visa fee - 553 MDL(paid at bank)
Declaration Form (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)
Declaration Form (English)
Declaration Form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/israel/moldova/english/pdf/Declaration-template-%D0%A0%D0%A3.pdf) (Russian)
Declaration Form (Romanian)
visa_types.processing_time: - Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Visitor visa is granted to someone who wishes to stay in Israel for only a short time (for a visit, tourism, or visiting relatives and friends). A person who enters Israel on a Tourist visa is not allowed to work in the State of Israel.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Visitor visa is granted to someone who wishes to stay in Israel for only a short time (for a visit, tourism, or visiting relatives and friends). A person who enters Israel on a Tourist visa is not allowed to work in the State of Israel. 

## Visa Fees And Service Charge
Fees are due on the visa application when applying.

[B2 Tourist Visit > visa fees]
Visa fees payments can only be made in local currency (MDL) by credit card at the VFS Israel Visa Application Centre.
- VFS Service Charges are inclusive of taxes and are non-refundable.
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Consular services

## Documents Required
Required documents:
- A completed and signed visa application form (Form K/1).
- Two recent color photographs, size 5×5 cm, on a white background.
- Original passport of the applicant + a copy of the passport.
- Medical insurance issued in the applicant’s name, covering the entire period of stay in Israel + payment receipt.
- Round-trip flight tickets issued in the applicant’s name.
- Employment certificate for the last months / letter from the employer.
- Bank statement showing account transactions for the last three months.
- Proof of accommodation in Israel:
- 
– hotel reservation, or
- – confirmation of accommodation from friends/relatives (indicating the address in Israel and supporting documents / invitation letter with the address).
- Contact details of relatives or friends in Israel (if applicable).
Consular visa fee - 553 MDL(paid at bank)

## Photo Specifications
- Recent colour passport photos, against a white background, unobstructed face, 5 x 5 cm.

## Processing Time
- Standard visa processing time is 7 working days from the day of receipt of the application at the Embassy. In case the documents are not submitted as per requirements or due to other factors or justified reasons, the processing time may be extended.
